diagrams.title: Python File Object Hierarchy
diagrams.description: Shows the class hierarchy of Python file objects including TextIOWrapper, BufferedReader, and their methods. Helps students understand how different file types are handled internally.
elements: File Methods: read(), write(), readline(), seek(), tell()
File modes: r, w, a, rb, wb
diagrams.purpose: Explains the object-oriented structure behind Python's file handling and the difference between text and binary file operations

diagrams.title: Context Manager Protocol for Files
diagrams.description: Illustrates the flow of the 'with' statement showing __enter__ and __exit__ methods, automatic resource cleanup, and exception handling in file operations.
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrates the OOP context manager pattern for safe file handling and proper resource management

diagrams.title: Pathlib Class Methods Overview
diagrams.description: Shows the pathlib.Path class with its key methods for path manipulation, file checking, and content reading/writing in an object-oriented manner.
diagrams.purpose: Introduces the modern OOP approach to file system paths using pathlib instead of os.path string manipulation
